text: IRD Sri Lanka Employer Responsibilities (PAYE). This page summarizes employer obligations under the PAYE scheme, including document retention, remittance deadlines, employee certificates, annual declarations, employer liability, penalties, special tax-table situations, and contact expectations when PAYE tables do not apply or clarification is needed.

Employer responsibilities under the PAYE scheme listed by IRD: keep in safe custody the documents relating to every payment made to employees and make those documents available whenever officers authorized by the Commissioner General call for inspection under section 119; remit to the Commissioner General every tax deduction made under the PAYE scheme during a month not later than the 15th day of the immediately following month under section 120(a); issue to all employees details of the tax deductions made during the year in the Certificate of Tax Deduction, Form T10, before the expiry of April 30 in the following year under section 120(b); furnish the Annual Declaration to the Commissioner General not later than April 30 every year under section 120(d).

subsection: Employer liability, penalties, special tax-table situations, and contact guidance
text: The page states that employers are responsible for recovery and or remittance of taxes, and employers who fail to do so are personally liable to pay the amount under section 124(1). In addition to the tax, a penalty of an amount not exceeding 50 percent of the tax may apply in case of failure to deduct and or remit tax. The page also says the tax referred to in Table 6 should be paid in addition to the tax referred to in Tables 1, 2, 4, 5, and 7 when an employer or any other person settles an employee's income tax without deducting it from salary, or when the employer reimburses income tax already deducted from the employee's salary and remitted to the Inland Revenue Department. Employers are required to contact the Commissioner PAYE or the Secretariat if no tax table in the PAYE Tax Tables applies to a payment made to an employee or if further clarification is needed.
